description: Narrow exceptions for legacy machine contracts and immutable upstream provenance. Entries are ordered from specific to broad; expectedOccurrences locks each exception against growth.

entries.reason: The audit policy must spell the legacy identities it classifies.
entries.removeWhen: Remove each token only after its final compatibility or provenance entry is retired.

entries.reason: The scanner must recognize every frozen legacy identity.
entries.removeWhen: Remove a token after its compatibility and provenance contracts no longer exist.

entries.reason: The upstream package map and repository link are immutable fork lineage.
entries.removeWhen: Never while this repository derives from the recorded upstream baseline.

entries.reason: Exact upstream-to-downstream mappings are required for artifact normalization and attribution.
entries.removeWhen: Never while the fork publishes artifacts derived from this upstream baseline.

entries.reason: Published upstream changelog entries are historical release evidence.
entries.removeWhen: Never; append downstream release notes instead of rewriting released history.

entries.reason: Captured goal audit logs, patches, and reports must remain verbatim.
entries.removeWhen: Never; these files are immutable audit evidence.

entries.reason: The captured evidence paths retain the upstream directory seam.
entries.removeWhen: Only during a separately reviewed repository path migration.

entries.reason: The remediation record documents upstream behavior and commands.
entries.removeWhen: Never; supersede it with a new report.

entries.reason: The deep-audit output is historical evidence rather than current product copy.
entries.removeWhen: Never; generate a new dated audit instead.

entries.reason: The upstream-sync directory remains packages/ggcoder while the npm package is @kleio/coder.
entries.removeWhen: A coordinated git mv updates build scripts, source links, audits, and upstream-sync tooling.

entries.reason: The upstream-sync directory remains packages/gg-boss while the npm package is @kleio/manager.
entries.removeWhen: A coordinated git mv updates build scripts, source links, audits, and upstream-sync tooling.

entries.reason: This non-spine package and its directory are outside the five-package release unit.
entries.removeWhen: The package receives its own mapped, versioned rename release.

entries.reason: Published bridge subpaths are wire and import contracts.
entries.removeWhen: A separately versioned @kleio/voice migration ships aliases for every bridge subpath.

entries.reason: The atomic version check maps branded package names to retained upstream-sync directories.
entries.removeWhen: The corresponding repository directories are migrated.

entries.reason: These active dependencies and non-spine package names have no owned @kleio publication yet.
entries.removeWhen: Owned replacement packages are published and clean-consumer tests pass against them.

entries.reason: Lockfile resolutions must continue to match active upstream dependency coordinates.
entries.removeWhen: The corresponding owned replacement package is adopted and the lockfile is regenerated.

entries.reason: Runtime imports must use the coordinates of packages that actually exist.
entries.removeWhen: Owned replacement packages are published and adopted in one reviewed migration.

entries.reason: The bundled installer still fetches skills from this verified external repository.
entries.removeWhen: An owned mirror exists and its contents and redirect behavior are verified.

entries.reason: Go and Swift module URLs are package-resolution contracts.
entries.removeWhen: Owned repositories, module tags, and consumer migration guidance exist.

entries.reason: OpenAI originator, user-agent, and prompt-cache identities affect routing and cache continuity.
entries.removeWhen: A live provider contract and cache migration prove branded values safe.

entries.reason: ErrorSource ggcoder is a serialized machine discriminator, not a display label.
entries.removeWhen: All consumers accept a branded discriminator and persisted errors have a dual-reader migration.

entries.reason: Voice tool names, request IDs, public types, and bridge routes are published contracts.
entries.removeWhen: A separately versioned @kleio/voice release provides additive branded aliases and dual routing.

entries.reason: Generated snippets and idempotency markers persist in user projects and must remain readable.
entries.removeWhen: A dual-reader recognizes old and branded markers before a branded writer ships.

entries.reason: Legacy executable names must continue to resolve to the branded CLI entry points.
entries.removeWhen: A future major release completes a documented command migration and deprecation period.

entries.reason: GGBoss and GGBossOptions are published TypeScript and JavaScript exports.
entries.removeWhen: Only after the complete 4.10.1-kleio.* line and a later major-version deprecation window.

entries.reason: ProjectSource ggcoder is a machine value shared by discovery and UI mapping.
entries.removeWhen: Persisted and cross-process project-source consumers support a branded discriminator.

entries.reason: The Manager prompt-cache prefix preserves provider cache continuity.
entries.removeWhen: A measured cache migration accepts an intentional cold-cache cutover.

entries.reason: Persisted truncated tool results use this exact idempotency marker.
entries.removeWhen: A dual-reader recognizes both markers before a new writer value ships.

entries.reason: APP_NAME remains a legacy public export while the product profile supplies branded display names.
entries.removeWhen: A later major version removes the documented legacy export.

entries.reason: The Coder prompt-cache prefix preserves provider cache continuity and downgrade behavior.
entries.removeWhen: A measured cache migration accepts an intentional cold-cache cutover.

entries.reason: Agent Home relay and mobile associations key registrations by the immutable ggcoder ID.
entries.removeWhen: Relay and mobile clients ship a coordinated alias and association migration.

entries.reason: The contract test proves the legacy executable and APP_NAME export remain available beside Kleio Coder.
entries.removeWhen: A later major release removes both legacy contracts after a documented deprecation period.

entries.reason: Pixel and subprocess command resolution retains the installed legacy executable as the final fallback.
entries.removeWhen: The legacy executable completes its major-version deprecation window and external automation has migrated.

entries.reason: The test locks the legacy Coder prompt-cache prefix and its emitted key format.
entries.removeWhen: A measured cache migration accepts an intentional cold-cache cutover.

entries.reason: The test locks startup, shutdown, and component strings consumed by existing log filters.
entries.removeWhen: Structured product fields replace legacy string filters and cleanup supports both generations.

entries.reason: Pasted legacy MCP add commands remain accepted and are covered by parser tests.
entries.removeWhen: A later major release ends the documented pasted-command compatibility window.

entries.reason: The tests prove an explicit or configured legacy MCP client identity remains supported.
entries.removeWhen: Known MCP servers no longer require the legacy client identity and a migration is documented.

entries.reason: The registration test proves existing relay and mobile associations keep their machine ID.
entries.removeWhen: Relay and mobile clients ship a coordinated alias and association migration.

entries.reason: The tests prove strict endpoints can explicitly request the previous HTTP user-agent identity.
entries.removeWhen: Endpoint compatibility evidence shows the legacy override is no longer needed.

entries.reason: The deprecated ggcoderBin option, fallback tests, and temporary-name seam remain compatible with existing automation.
entries.removeWhen: A later major release removes the option after callers migrate and cleanup handles both temporary prefixes.

entries.reason: Existing launch agents, OAuth setups, audio helpers, and tests consume these environment names.
entries.removeWhen: All branded aliases have shipped with legacy fallback for a documented deprecation period.

entries.reason: Legacy logger components and prefixes remain stable for existing filters and debug tooling.
entries.removeWhen: Structured product fields replace string filters and cleanup supports both generations.

entries.reason: The profile centralizes legacy command, HTTP, MCP, and Agent Home identifiers beside preferred values.
entries.removeWhen: Each downstream contract has completed its separately reviewed migration and deprecation window.
